Predicted seagrass carbon map at Ria de Aveiro for 2024 and 2025

Lee, Chengfa Benjamin

2026-03-05

This page documents the results of the Zostera noltii seagrass carbon mapping work at Ria de Aviero. This includes work done in:

  • The fieldtrip of Aveiro 2025 by Simon Oiry
  • Coring Carbon Seagrass by Simon Oiry
  • Exploring the relationship between seagrass reflectance and biomass

All analyses were performed in R version 4.5.2 (R Core Team 2025). All work belong to the REWRITE project.

1 Study Location

This page focuses on Ria de Aveiro, a demonstrator site for the REWRITE PROJECT. A natural site and rewilded site was designated in the lagoon and seagrass meadows were found in both sites. Its seagrass meadow was estimated to be 3.52 km2 during the peak season of 2024 (Davies et al. 2024).

The sites were sampled according to a grid, and additional stations were sampled randomly within the grid.

4 Carbon stock prediction

Table 4.1: Seagrass biomass and carbon at Ria de Aveiro. “DW” denotes the dry weight of the seagrass samples while “C” denotes the carbon in the seagrass samples.

(m2)		 Total biomass per area
(kg DW/ha)		 Total biomass
(kg DW)		 Carbon content
(g C/g DW)		 Carbon stock per area
(kg C/m2)		 Total carbon stock
(ton)

Davies, Bede Ffinian Rowe, Simon Oiry, Philippe Rosa, Maria Laura Zoffoli, Ana I. Sousa, Oliver R. Thomas, Dan A. Smale, et al. 2024. “Intertidal Seagrass Extent from Sentinel-2 Time-Series Show Distinct Trajectories in Western Europe.” Remote Sensing of Environment 312: 114340. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rse.2024.114340.
R Core Team. 2025. R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing. Vienna, Austria: R Foundation for Statistical Computing. https://www.R-project.org/.
